title: Crowded Campground With Worthy Views
campsites: Red Canyon of Wind River Dispersed
summary:
Another rainy day in the forecast meant another day trying to find some hot springs. We stopped at Thermopolis Hot Springs, which offers a nice free hot springs pool and a shower. Interestingly, this is because of a 1896 treaty with the Shoshone and Arapaho tribes, which gave Wyoming the hot springs on the condition that they remain free and accessible to the public. We managed to get out of town just in time, as big raindrops and high winds started chasing us. We stopped at a roadside pull off for the night, made some dolled up ramen and binged the show Slow Horses.

The next morning we got up and out early to try to get to Jackson in time to get some dispersed camping. Luckily, the first road we went up had some available spots and we were able to snag a designated spot with beautiful views of the Tetons. Colin went on a run and I went on a nice walk with Maya and then we spent the afternoon reading in the sun. Colin made delicious quesadillas to end the day.

photos.caption: The view of the Tetons from our dispersed campsite outside Jackson.
A view of the Snake River from Colin's run.
